overview: From 2026-06-26 to 2026-06-27, elizaos/eliza had 71 new PRs (72 merged), 0 new issues, and 9 active contributors.

topPRs.title: feat(app): harden desktop shell and view apps
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

- Harden desktop/web shell view switching, app-control view commands, plugin lifecycle/view registry behavior, and cloud/dev launch paths.
- Add simple Notes/Calendar test views plus calendar LifeOps HTTP routes so view switching and view-backed actions have concrete plugin surfaces to exercise.
- Polish launcher UX: home <-> springboard and inner springboard pages now follow the pointer during swipe, then settle with a spring-like transition; springboard tiles render clean deterministic app icons instead of cropped preview/hero images.
- Expand regression coverage for continuous chat gestures, shell layout stability, springboard interaction, view switching, app-control, calendar routes, plugin loading, and dev smoke helpers.

## Why

This branch packages the local desktop/cloud shell QA work into one draft PR so maintainers can review it against current `develop`. The main user-facing issues addressed are laggy/snap-only launcher paging, messy app icons, fragile chat/shell gesture states, and missing concrete test plugin views for notes/calendar style workflows.

## Impact

- UI users should see smoother launcher paging and cleaner app icons across browser, desktop, and mobile webviews.
- App-control can target and reason about view context more reliably.
- Calendar/simple view plugin surfaces give QA and agents real view-backed actions instead of fake-only paths.
- Calendar unit config now aliases source workspace packages consistently and keeps real-db tests out of the unit lane.

## Checks

- `bunx @biomejs/biome check packages/ui/src/components/shell/HomeSpringboardSurface.tsx packages/ui/src/components/shell/HomeSpringboardSurface.composed.test.tsx packages/ui/src/components/views/ViewTileImage.tsx packages/ui/src/components/pages/Springboard.tsx packages/ui/src/components/pages/Springboard.test.tsx plugins/plugin-calendar/vitest.config.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui test -- src/components/shell/ContinuousChatOverlay.test.tsx src/components/shell/ContinuousChatOverlay.fuzz.test.tsx src/components/shell/HomeSpringboardSurface.composed.test.tsx src/components/pages/Springboard.test.tsx src/components/shell/use-pull-gesture.test.ts src/hooks/view-catalog.test.ts src/hooks/useLayoutShiftMonitor.test.ts src/testing/layout-stability.test.ts src/App.navigate-view-wiring.test.tsx src/app-navigate-view.test.ts`
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-simple-views test`
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-calendar test`
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control test -- src/actions/view-command-matcher.test.ts src/actions/views-switching.test.ts src/evaluators/view-context.test.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e -- test/ui-smoke/springboard-interaction.spec.ts`
- Manual Playwright rendered `/apps` mobile and desktop: launcher swipe state followed pointer, app-page swipe followed pointer, no console/page errors observed.

topPRs.title: fix(training): move SFT builder to active 2b tier
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Summary:
- move the real benchmark-aligned Eliza-1 SFT dataset builder to `build_eliza1_sft_2b.py`
- update the builder manifest/output contract from retired `eliza1-sft-0_8b` / `eliza-1-0_8b` to active `eliza1-sft-2b` / `eliza-1-2b`
- point the full-corpus builder at `datasets/eliza1-sft-2b`
- leave the old `build_eliza1_sft_0_6b.py` and `build_eliza1_sft_0_8b.py` commands as compatibility wrappers that call the active 2B builder

Evidence:
- `rg -n "0_8b|0\.8B|eliza-1-0_8b|eliza1-sft-0_8b|0_6b|eliza-1-0_6b|eliza1-sft-0_6b" packages/training/scripts/build_eliza1_sft_2b.py packages/training/scripts/build_eliza1_fullcorpus.py` returned no matches
- `uv run pytest tests/test_apollo_default.py scripts/test_inagent_eliza_tiers.py scripts/test_train_nebius_smoke_all_tiers.py` passed: 23 passed
- `uv run python -m py_compile scripts/build_eliza1_sft_2b.py scripts/build_eliza1_sft_0_6b.py scripts/build_eliza1_sft_0_8b.py scripts/build_eliza1_fullcorpus.py`
- `git diff --check`
- `git fetch origin`
- `git rebase origin/develop`
- `bun install` completed with no package changes
- `bun run verify` passed: 530 successful, 530 total; build/typecheck audit passed

UI/media/LLM evidence: N/A, training script entrypoint/output-contract cleanup only.

topPRs.title: build: consolidate model-provider build.ts onto a shared driver (#9626)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Part of #9626 (TL;DR #2 — "58 hand-rolled `build.ts` reimplement one algorithm"). Starts the consolidation with the model-provider family the audit called out as "near-identical copies."

Adds **`plugins/plugin-build.ts`**, a config-driven driver that owns the shared orchestration (optional dist clean via `rm-path-recursive.mjs`, per-target `Bun.build`, post-build renames, `tsc` declaration emit, and the alias shims). Converts the 8 model-provider `build.ts` to small declarative `buildPlugin({...})` calls listing only what each differs on: **anthropic, openai, google-genai, groq, xai, openrouter, nearai, zai**.

**Net −488 LOC** (957 → 469 including the new shared driver).

### Evidence — every conversion is byte-identical
For each of the 8: built the **original** `build.ts` → captured `dist/`, then built the **driver** version → captured `dist/`, then `diff -rq` over the full tree (`.js`, `.js.map`, `.d.ts`). **Zero differences across all 8**, including the tricky cases:
- groq's cycle-avoidance root alias (`./node/index.node`, no node shim),
- openai's single-quoted shims + minified browser,
- xai's `index.ts`-bundle + `index.js`→`index.node.*` rename workaround (Bun codegen bug),
- openrouter's `linked` sourcemap + Bun `naming` + hand-written declarations (no tsc).

`audit:build-model` and `audit:turbo-build-deps` still pass; the driver typechecks clean. Subsumes the dist-clean hardening (#9626 finding #8) for these 8 packages.

The remaining ~50 connector `build.ts` can follow the same verified pattern incrementally (several are mid-migration in the concurrent dist-clean work, so they're intentionally left for a later batch to avoid conflicts).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: fix(computeruse): repair macOS window listing; complete #9581 on-device CUA input coverage (9/9)
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## What

Completes the first remaining item of #9581 — **macOS on-device CUA verification to full input coverage** — and fixes the two real macOS bugs that were blocking it.

The macOS desktop CUA evidence harness was stuck at **6/9** with `accessibilityPermission`, `mouseKeyboardInput`, and `windowListFocus` reported as `requires_device_evidence`. They were **not** actually blocked on a permission — `AXIsProcessTrusted` / System Events `UI elements enabled` were `true` the whole time. They were blocked by two genuine bugs in `windows-list.ts`.

## Root causes + fixes (`plugins/plugin-computeruse/src/platform/windows-list.ts`)

1. **macOS window listing was 100% broken.** `listWindowsDarwin` / `getActiveWindow` read `(id of w as text)`, but System Events `window` elements have **no `id`** — reading it throws `-1728`/`-1700` for every window, the surrounding `try` swallowed it, and `listWindows()` returned `[]` on every macOS host. So `get_window_position` / `switch_to_window` failed with "Window not found", which the harness mis-attributed to missing Accessibility.
   - Now enumerated via `CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo` (Swift, ~0.5s incl. compile; needs only Screen Recording for titles) with a System Events fallback for hosts without the Swift toolchain. Windows are identified by title scoped to the owning app — the only term the process-level AppleScript targeting can act on.

2. **`runDarwinWindowScript` timed out + was mis-classified.** It walked `every window of` **every non-matching process** through the accessibility tree before reaching the target; on a busy desktop that blew the 5s `osascript` timeout, and `ETIMEDOUT` matches the accessibility-error classifier → bogus "Accessibility denied".
   - Now a two-pass match: process name first (fast, no AX walk), window title only as fallback.

## Harness robustness (`scripts/capture-macos-desktop-evidence.mjs`)

Directly after synthetic keyboard input, TextEdit's **application** Apple Event dispatch (`get text of front document`) is transiently blocked and hangs to its timeout — even though the typed text is already present in the **accessibility tree**. The input proof now reads the text-area AX `value` (exactly what a CUA agent observes on screen), keeping the document read only as a fallback; the cleanup close retries past the same transient block so runs don't leak stale windows.

## Evidence — 9/9 `passed`

Captured on Apple **M4 Max** (`Mac16,5`, macOS `26.2` build `25C56`):

```
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse capture:macos-desktop-evidence   # status: passed
bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse validate:platform-evidence -- \
  ../../.github/issue-evidence/9581-macos-desktop-cua/macos-desktop-validation.json --require-complete
# → [macos-desktop-evidence] 9 checks validated (passed)
```

All nine checks pass; `accessibilityPermission`, `mouseKeyboardInput`, `windowListFocus` flip to `passed`. Artifacts under `.github/issue-evidence/9581-macos-desktop-cua/` include a **screen recording** (`input-proof.mp4`) of the agent driving a controlled TextEdit window (`mouse_move → click → key_combo(cmd+a) → type`, AX-verified) plus the validated manifest/report and `NOTES.md`.

## Tests

- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse typecheck` — clean.
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse test` — green; added `parseDarwinWindowOutput` regression tests guarding the owner|||title parse so the empty-list bug can't silently return.

Refs #9581.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: test(cloud-e2e): cover the multi-turn agent conversation journey
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
Adds a cloud-e2e spec for the biggest uncovered journey on the mock stack: a **multi-turn agent conversation**.

## What it covers (mock stack, no secrets)
seeded user -> create a cloud agent (`POST /api/v1/eliza/agents`) -> provision to running (control-plane mock tick) -> send a first message -> assert an inference response + token usage recorded -> send a SECOND message that depends on the first turn context -> assert the response reflects retained context + the conversation/message history persisted -> (monetized) per-turn credit deduction.

`packages/test/cloud-e2e/tests/agent-multi-turn-conversation.spec.ts` (+308, **no `test.skip`** — it runs for real). Fixtures extended: `mock-llm.ts` (context-aware mock responses across turns), `stack.ts`, `seed-pricing.ts`.

## Why
The showcase/monetized loops cover deploy+billing but nothing exercised a real multi-turn chat with retained context + per-turn metering — this fills that gap and runs per-PR via the `tests/*.spec.ts` glob.

## Verification
Runs on the in-process PGlite mock stack (no creds). Relying on CI (`cloud-e2e.yml`) to confirm green — the heavy local mock-stack run was fragile in my env; flagging so a reviewer can confirm the run output before merge.

completedItems.title: fix(feed): cut feed-web build memory so it fits Railway's builder
completedItems.body:
## Problem
`feed-web` deploys on Railway **hung indefinitely** in `next build` (page-data / static generation). The build set `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` on an ~8 GB builder → **zero headroom → Node thrash/stall**. The hang poi

completedItems.body: Continues the `build.ts` consolidation (TL;DR #2) from #9826, completing the model/TTS-provider family the audit flagged as "near-identical copies": **lmstudio, ollama, embeddings, edge-tts, elevenlabs, anthropic-proxy** (14 providers total

topContributors.summary: NubsCarson: Drove significant stability and functionality improvements across the elizaos/eliza codebase by merging 9 pull requests focused on critical bug fixes, including resolving model-slot leaks (#9817), refining web-lookup helpers (#9775), and polishing desktop chat navigation (#9791). Their work was heavily concentrated on bug-fixing and testing, accounting for 83% of their total activity and resulting in 7566 lines of code additions.

odilitime: Focused on optimizing CI infrastructure by migrating trusted CodeQL runs to robot runners in elizaos/eliza (#9815) and managing ongoing aggregate failure workflows. Their work primarily centered on testing and configuration updates, involving modifications across 41 files to improve project stability.

standujar: Focused on enhancing cloud infrastructure and testing reliability, successfully merging two PRs in elizaos/eliza that corrected cloud sign-in routing (#9813) and implemented comprehensive multi-turn agent conversation testing (#9814). Their work was primarily dedicated to bugfix efforts, which accounted for 83% of their total code contributions across 61 modified files.

lalalune: Drove a comprehensive architectural transition across the elizaos/eliza repository by consolidating model-provider build scripts (#9826, #9828) and systematically aligning training, inference, and mobile fixtures with the Gemma model tier. Their extensive work involved merging 53 PRs that modernized the codebase by removing retired Qwen dependencies, optimizing CI/CD build processes, and repairing macOS window listing functionality (#9820), demonstrating a strong focus on build system efficiency and model integration stability.

noobshow: Focused on improving system reliability by resolving a critical bug in elizaos/eliza via PR #9818, which deferred environment account materialization to ensure more stable service initialization. This effort involved a significant refactor across 177 files, underscoring a dedicated focus on core bugfix work.

axiosdevs: Expanded the ecosystem by integrating third-party functionality into the elizaos/eliza repository via PR #9811. This contribution focused on configuration updates to support the new plugin-agentscoin.
